// Clear previous captures\n297\t    state.capture.clear().await;\n298\t\n299\t    Ok(Json(CaptureResponse {\n300\t        capture_id,\n301\t        queries: Vec::new(),\n302\t    }))\n303\t}\n304\t\n305\t/// Get captured queries\n306\tpub async fn get_captured<S>(\n307\t    State(state): State<CanaryState>,\n308\t) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>,    pub fn new(\n112\t        store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n113\t        max_concurrent: usize,\n114\t        run_history_limit: usize,\n115\t        search_executor: SearchExecutor,\n116\t tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(5));\n133\t\n134\t        loop {\n135\t            interval.tick().await;\n136\t            if let Err(e) = self.run_due_canaries().await {\n137\t                tracing::error!(\"Canary runner error: {}\", e);\n138\t            }\n139\t        }\n140\t    }\n141\t\n142\t    /// Run all canaries that are due\n143\t    async fn run_due_canaries(&self) -> Result<()> {\n144\t        let canaries = self.store.list_canaries()?;\n145\t        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n146\t\n147\t        // Filter enabled canaries that are due\n148\t        let mut due_canaries = Vec::new();\n149\t        for canary in canaries {\n150\t            if !canary.enabled {\n151\t                continue;\n152\t            }\n153\t\n154\t            // Check if already running\n155\t            let running = self.running.read().await;\n156\t            if running.get(&canary.id).copied().unwrap_or(false) {\n157\t                continue;\n158\t            }\n159\t            drop(running);\n160\t\n161\t            // Check last run time\n162\t            let runs = self.store.get_canary_runs(&canary.id, 1)?;\n163\t            let should_run = if let Some(last_run) = runs.first() {\n164\t                let elapsed_ms = now - last_run.ran_at;\n165\t                elapsed_ms >= (canary.interval_s * 1000)\n166\t            } else {\n167\t                true\n168\t            };\n169\t\n170\t            if should_run {\n171\t                due_canaries.push(canary);\n172\t            }\n173\t        }\n174\t\n175\t        // Run up to max_concurrent canaries\n176\t        for canary in due_canaries.into_iter().take(self.max_concurrent) {\n177\t            let canary_id = canary.id.clone();\n178\t            let runner = self.clone_runner();\n179\t\n180\t            // Mark as running\n181\t            {\n182\t                let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n183\t                running.insert(canary_id.clone(), true);\n184\t            }\n185\t\n186\t            tokio::spawn(async move {\n187\t                let result = runner.run_canary(&canary).await;\n188\t\n189\t                // Mark as not running\n190\t                {\n191\t                    let mut running = runner.running.write().await;\n192\t                    running.remove(&canary_id);\n193\t                }\n194\t\n195\t                if let Err(e) = result {\n196\t                    tracing::error!(\"Canary {} failed: {}\", canary_id, e);\n197\t                }\n198\t            });\n199\t        }\n200\t\n201\t        Ok(())\n202\t    }\n203\t\n204\t    /// Run a single canary\n205\t    async fn run_canary(&self, canary: &CanaryRow) -> Result<CanaryRunResult> {\n206\t        let start = serde_json::from_str(&canary.query_json)\n211\t            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary query: {}\", e)))?;\n212\t\n213\t        // Parse assertions\n214\t e)))?;\n216\t\n217\t        // Execute the search query against the index\n218\t        // Note: This would need to be wired to the actual search client\n219\t        // For now, we simulate a search };\n247\t\n248\t        // Store the run\n249\t        let new_run = NewCanaryRun {\n250\t            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n251\t            ran_at: now,\n252\t            status:             None\n256\t            } else {\n257\t                Some(serde_json::to_string(&result.failed_assertions).unwrap_or_default())\n258\t            },\n259\t        };\n260\t\n261\t        self.store.insert_canary_run(&new_run, self.run_history_limit)?;\n262\t\n263\t        // Emit metrics\n264\t        self.emit_metrics(&result);\n265\t\n266\t        Ok(result)\n267\t    }\n401\t        }\n402\t    }\n403\t\n404\t    /// Clone the runner for use in spawned tasks\n405\t    fn clone_runner(&self) -> Self {\n406\t        Self {\n407\t            store: index_uid: String, query: SearchQuery, response: SearchResponse) {\n467\t        let mut queries = self.queries.write().await;\n468\t        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n469\t\n470\t        queries.push(CapturedQuery {\n471\t            index_uid,\n472\t            query,\n473\t            response,\n474\t            timestamp: now,\n475\t        });\n476\t\n477\t        // Keep only the most recent queries\n478\t        let len = queries.len();\n479\t        if len > self.max_queries {\n480\t            queries.drain(0..len - self.max_queries);\n481\t        }\n482\t    }\n483\t\n484\t    /// Get captured queries\n485\t    pub async fn get_captured(&self) -> Vec<CapturedQuery> {\n486\t §13.18 Synthetic canary queries with golden assertions\n\nuse crate::{\n    error::{MiroirError, Result},\n    task_store::{NewCanary, NewCanaryRun, CanaryRow, TaskStore},\n};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Canary assertion types\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum CanaryAssertion {\n    TopHitId { value: String },\n    TopKContains { k: usize, ids: Vec<String> },\n    MinHits { value: usize },\n    MaxP95Ms { value: u64 },\n    SettingsVersionAtLeast { value: i64 },\n    MustNotContainId { id: String },\n}\n\n/// Canary definition\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Canary {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub name: String,\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub interval_s: i64,\n    pub query: SearchQuery,\n    pub assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion>,\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    pub created_at: i64,\n}\n\n/// Search query for canary\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchQuery {\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub params: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Canary run result\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CanaryRunResult {\n    pub canary_id: String,\n    pub ran_at: i64,\n    pub status: CanaryStatus,\n    pub latency_ms: i64,\n    pub failed_assertions: Vec<AssertionFailure>,\n    pub hit_count: usize,\n    pub top_hit_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Canary run status\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum CanaryStatus {\n    Passed,\n    Failed,\n    Error,\n}\n\n/// Assertion failure details\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct AssertionFailure {\n    pub assertion_type: String,\n    pub expected: serde_json::Value,\n    pub actual: serde_json::Value,\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\n/// Search response from Meilisearch\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchResponse {\n    pub hits: Vec<Hit>,\n    pub estimated_total_hits: usize,\n    pub processing_time_ms: usize,\n    pub query: String,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Hit {\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub fields: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Search executor callback for canary queries.\npub type SearchExecutor = Arc<dyn Fn(&str, &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Metrics emitter callback for canary runs.\npub type MetricsEmitter = Arc<dyn Fn(&CanaryRunResult) + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Settings version checker callback.\npub type SettingsVersionChecker = Arc<dyn Fn(&str) -> Option<i64> + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Canary runner\npub struct CanaryRunner {\n    store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    running: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, bool>>>,\n    max_concurrent: usize,\n    run_history_limit: usize,\n    search_executor: SearchExecutor,\n    metrics_emitter: MetricsEmitter,\n    settings_version_checker: SettingsVersionChecker,\n}\n\nimpl CanaryRunner {\n    pub fn new(\n        store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        max_concurrent: usize,\n        run_history_limit: usize,\n        search_executor: SearchExecutor,\n        metrics_emitter: MetricsEmitter,\n        settings_version_checker: SettingsVersionChecker,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            store,\n            running: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            max_concurrent,\n            run_history_limit,\n            search_executor,\n            metrics_emitter,\n            settings_version_checker,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background canary runner\n    pub async fn start(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(5));\n\n        loop {\n            interval.tick().await;\n            if let Err(e) = self.run_due_canaries().await {\n                tracing::error!(\"Canary runner error: {}\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Run all canaries that are due\n    async fn run_due_canaries(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let canaries = self.store.list_canaries()?;\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Filter enabled canaries that are due\n        let mut due_canaries = Vec::new();\n        for canary in canaries {\n            if !canary.enabled {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check if already running\n            let running = self.running.read().await;\n            if running.get(&canary.id).copied().unwrap_or(false) {\n                continue;\n            }\n            drop(running);\n\n            // Check last run time\n            let runs = self.store.get_canary_runs(&canary.id, 1)?;\n            let should_run = if let Some(last_run) = runs.first() {\n                let elapsed_ms = now - last_run.ran_at;\n                elapsed_ms >= (canary.interval_s * 1000)\n            } else {\n                true\n            };\n\n            if should_run {\n                due_canaries.push(canary);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Run up to max_concurrent canaries\n        for canary in due_canaries.into_iter().take(self.max_concurrent) {\n            let canary_id = canary.id.clone();\n            let runner = self.clone_runner();\n\n            // Mark as running\n            {\n                let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n                running.insert(canary_id.clone(), true);\n            }\n\n            tokio::spawn(async move {\n                let result = runner.run_canary(&canary).await;\n\n                // Mark as not running\n                {\n                    let mut running = runner.running.write().await;\n                    running.remove(&canary_id);\n                }\n\n                if let Err(e) = result {\n                    tracing::error!(\"Canary {} failed: {}\", canary_id, e);\n                }\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Run a single canary\n    async fn run_canary(&self, canary: &CanaryRow) -> Result<CanaryRunResult> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Parse query\n        let query: SearchQuery = serde_json::from_str(&canary.query_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary query: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Parse assertions\n        let assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion> = serde_json::from_str(&canary.assertions_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary assertions: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Execute the search query against the index\n        // Note: This would need to be wired to the actual search client\n        // For now, we simulate a search response\n        let search_response = self.execute_search(&canary.index_uid, &query).await?;\n\n        let latency_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as i64;\n\n        // Evaluate assertions\n        let mut failed_assertions = Vec::new();\n        for assertion in &assertions {\n            if let Some(failure) = self.evaluate_assertion(&assertion, &search_response, latency_ms, &canary.index_uid) {\n                failed_assertions.push(failure);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let status = if failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed\n        } else {\n            CanaryStatus::Failed\n        };\n\n        let result = CanaryRunResult {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status,\n            latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions,\n            hit_count: search_response.hits.len(),\n            top_hit_id: search_response.hits.first().and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string())),\n        };\n\n        // Store the run\n        let new_run = NewCanaryRun {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status: serde_json::to_string(&result.status).unwrap_or_default(),\n            latency_ms: result.latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions_json: if result.failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some(serde_json::to_string(&result.failed_assertions).unwrap_or_default())\n            },\n        };\n\n        self.store.insert_canary_run(&new_run, self.run_history_limit)?;\n\n        // Emit metrics\n        self.emit_metrics(&result);\n\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a search query against the index\n    async fn execute_search(&self, index_uid: &str, query: &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> {\n        // Call the search executor callback\n        (self.search_executor)(index_uid, query)\n    }\n\n    /// Evaluate a single assertion\n    fn evaluate_assertion(\n        &self,\n        assertion: &CanaryAssertion,\n        response: &SearchResponse,\n        latency_ms: i64,\n        index_uid: &str,\n    ) -> Option<AssertionFailure> {\n        match assertion {\n            CanaryAssertion::TopHitId { value } => {\n                let top_hit_id = response.hits.first()\n                    .and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n\n                let actual = top_hit_id.unwrap_or(\"\");\n                if actual != value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_hit_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(actual),\n                        message: format!(\"Top hit ID mismatch: expected {}, got {}\", value, actual),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::TopKContains { k, ids } => {\n                let top_k_ids: Vec<&str> = response.hits.iter()\n                    .take(*k)\n                    .filter_map(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .collect();\n\n                let missing: Vec<_> = ids.iter()\n                    .filter(|id| !top_k_ids.contains(&id.as_str()))\n                    .collect();\n\n                if !missing.is_empty() {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_k_contains\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(ids),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(top_k_ids),\n                        message: format!(\"Top {} missing IDs: {:?}\", k, missing),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MinHits { value } => {\n                if response.hits.len() < *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"min_hits\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(response.hits.len()),\n                        message: format!(\"Hit count below minimum: {} < {}\", response.hits.len(), value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MaxP95Ms { value } => {\n                if latency_ms as u64 > *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"max_p95_ms\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(latency_ms),\n                        message: format!(\"Latency exceeded p95: {}ms > {}ms\", latency_ms, value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::SettingsVersionAtLeast { value } => {\n                // Get current settings version for the index\n                let current_version = (self.settings_version_checker)(index_uid)\n                    .unwrap_or(0);\n\n                if current_version < *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"settings_version_at_least\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(current_version),\n                        message: format!(\"Settings version below minimum: {} < {}\", current_version, value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MustNotContainId { id } => {\n                let contains = response.hits.iter()\n                    .any(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\")\n                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                        .map(|v| v == id.as_str())\n                        .unwrap_or(false));\n\n                if contains {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"must_not_contain_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(null),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(id),\n                        message: format!(\"Results contain forbidden ID: {}\", id),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Emit metrics for a canary run\n    fn emit_metrics(&self, result: &CanaryRunResult) {\n        // Call the metrics emitter callback\n        (self.metrics_emitter)(result);\n\n        // Also log for observability\n        match result.status {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed => {\n                tracing::info!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    \"Canary passed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Failed => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    failures = result.failed_assertions.len(),\n                    \"Canary failed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Error => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    \"Canary error\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Clone the runner for use in spawned tasks\n    fn clone_runner(&self) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            store: self.store.clone(),\n            running: self.running.clone(),\n            max_concurrent: self.max_concurrent,\n            run_history_limit: self.run_history_limit,\n            search_executor: self.search_executor.clone(),\n            metrics_emitter: self.metrics_emitter.clone(),\n            settings_version_checker: self.settings_version_checker.clone(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a canary from a definition\npub fn create_canary(\n    id: String,\n    name: String,\n    index_uid: String,\n    interval_s: i64,\n    query: SearchQuery,\n    assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion>,\n) -> Result<NewCanary> {\n    let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n    Ok(NewCanary {\n        id: id.clone(),\n        name,\n        index_uid,\n        interval_s,\n        query_json: serde_json::to_string(&query)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize query: {}\", e)))?,\n        assertions_json: serde_json::to_string(&assertions)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize assertions: {}\", e)))?,\n        enabled: true,\n        created_at: now,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Capture a query from traffic as a canary candidate\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CapturedQuery {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub query: SearchQuery,\n    pub response: SearchResponse,\n    pub timestamp: i64,\n}\n\npub struct QueryCapture {\n    queries: Arc<RwLock<Vec<CapturedQuery>>>,\n    max_queries: usize,\n}\n\nimpl QueryCapture {\n    pub fn new(max_queries: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            queries: Arc::new(RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n            max_queries,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Capture a query for canary creation\n    pub async fn capture(&self, index_uid: String, query: SearchQuery, response: SearchResponse) {\n        let mut queries = self.queries.write().await;\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        queries.push(CapturedQuery {\n            index_uid,\n            query,\n            response,\n            timestamp: now,\n        });\n\n        // Keep only the most recent queries\n        let len = queries.len();\n        if len > self.max_queries {\n            queries.drain(0..len - self.max_queries);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get captured queries\n    pub async fn get_captured(&self) -> Vec<CapturedQuery> {\n        self.queries.read().await.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear captured queries\n    pub async fn clear(&self) {\n        self.queries.write().await.clear();\n    }\n}\n","numLines":494,"startLine":1,"totalLines":494}}}

}\n203\t}\n204\t\n205\t#[tokio::main]\n206\tasync fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n207\t    // Load configuration (file → env → CLI overlay)\n208\t    let config = MiroirConfig::load()\n209\t        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Failed to load config: {}\", e))?;\n210\t\n211\t    // Initialize structured JSON logging (plan §10 format)\n212\t    // Fields on every line: timestamp, level, target, layer\n220\t    // Note: We rebuild the layers in each branch because the types differ\n221\t    // OTel layer must be applied to the bare registry first\n222\t    if let Some(otel_layer) = .with_target(true)\n227\t            .with_current_span(true)\n228\t            .with_span_list(false);\n229\t        // Apply OTel layer to registry first, then add filter and json layer\n230\t .with_span_list(false);\n242\t        registry()\n243\t            .with(filter)\n244\t            .with(json_layer)\n245\t            .init();\n246\t    }\n247\t\n248\t    // Set pod_id as a global default field so it appears on every log line.\n249\t    // This is done via a separate info span that is entered once and never\n250\t    // left — its fields propagate to all child spans and events.\n251\t    let _pod_span = tracing::info_span!(\"runtime\", pod_id = %pod_id).entered();\n252\t\n253\t    info!(\n254\t        shards = config.shards,\n255\t        replication_factor = \"orchestrator refuses to\n261\t    // start the search UI without it\").\n262\t    if config.search_ui.enabled {\n263\t        let jwt_env = &config.search_ui.auth.jwt_secret_env;\n264\t        match  }\n273\t        }\n274\t    }\n275\t\n276\t    // Build unified state\n277\t    let state = UnifiedState::new(config.clone());\n278\t\n279\t    // Start health checker background task\n280\t    let scoped_key_rotation::run_scoped_key_rotator(rotation_state).await;\n294\t        });\n295\t\n296\t        // Start admin session revocation Pub/Sub subscriber (plan §9).\n297\t        // When any pod revokes a session (logout), the session ID is published\n298\t        // to `miroir:admin_session:revoked`. Every pod subscribes and adds the\n299\t        // ID to its in-memory DashMap, ensuring revoked cookies are rejected\n300\t        // across all pods within milliseconds.\n301\t        let revoked_sessions = state.auth.revoked_sessions.clone();\n302\t        let revoked_total = state.auth.admin_session_revoked_total.clone();\n303\t        let redis_url = config.task_store.url.clone();\n304\t        let key_prefix = redis.key_prefix().to_string();\n305\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n306\t            info!(\"starting admin session revocation            move |session_id: String| {\n311\t                    revoked_sessions.insert(session_id, ());\n312\t                    revoked_total.inc();\n313\t                },\n314\t            )\n315\t            .await\n316\t            {\n317\t                error!(error = %e, \"admin session revocation subscriber exited with error\");\n318\t            }\n319\t        });\n320\t    }\n321\t\n322\t    // Build the main app router with UnifiedState\n323\t    let app = Router::new()\n324\t        .route(\"/health\", get(health::get_health))\n325\t        .route(\"/version\", get(version::get_version::<UnifiedState>))\n326\t        .route(\"/stats\", get(indexes::global_stats_handler))\n327\t        .route(\"/multi-search\", settings::router::<UnifiedState>())\n333\t        .nest(\"/tasks\", tasks::router::<UnifiedState>())\n334\t        // IMPORTANT: Layer order matters! Last layer() call = outermost = runs first.\n335\t        // The middleware stack (from outermost to innermost):\n336\t        // 1. csrf_middleware - runs first\n337\t        // 2. auth_middleware\n338\t        // 3. Extension layers\n339\t        // 4. request_id_middleware - sets X-Request-Id header\n340\t        // 5. telemetry_middleware - reads X-Request-Id, creates tracing span with request_id field\n341\t        //    The span's request_id field propagates to all child log events via with_current_span(true)\n342\t        //\n343\t        // To achieve this order, we add layers in REVERSE (last call = outermost):\n344\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n345\t            TelemetryState {\n346\t                metrics: state.metrics.clone(),\n347\t                pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n348\t            },\n349\t            middleware::telemetry_middleware,\n350\t        ))\n351\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n352\t            middleware::request_id_middleware,\n353\t        ))\n354\t        .layer(axum::extract::DefaultBodyLimit::max(\n355\t            config.server.max_body_bytes as usize,\n356\t        ))\n357\t        .layer(axum::Extension(state.admin.config.clone()))\n358\t        .layer(axum::Extension(std::sync::Arc::new(state.admin.clone())))\n359\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n360\t            state.auth.clone(),\n361\t            auth::auth_middleware,\n362\t        ))\n363\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n364\t            auth::CsrfState {\n365\t                auth: state.auth.clone(),\n366\t                redis_store: state.redis_store.clone(),\n367\t            },\n368\t            auth::csrf_middleware,\n369\t        ))\n370\t        .with_state(state.clone());\n371\t\n372\t    let main_addr: SocketAddr = format!(\"{}:{}\", config.server.bind, config.server.port)\n373\t        .parse()\n374\t        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Invalid bind address: {}\", e))?;\n375\t    let metrics_addr: SocketAddr = SocketAddr::from(([0, 0, 0, 0], 9090));\n376\t\n377\t    info!(\n378\t        main_addr = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(main_addr).await?;\n385\t    let metrics_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(metrics_addr).await?;\n386\t\n387\t    // Spawn main server with graceful shutdown\n388\t    let main_server = axum::serve(main_listener, app)\n389\t        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n390\t\n391\t    // Spawn metrics server with graceful shutdown\n392\t    let metrics_app = metrics_router().with_state(state.metrics.clone());\n393\t    let metrics_server = axum::serve(metrics_listener, metrics_app)\n394\t        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n395\t\n396\t    // Run both servers concurrently\n397\t    let (main_result, metrics_result) = tokio::join!(\n398\t        main_server,\n399\t        metrics_server\n400\t    );\n401\t\n402\t    // Check for errors\n403\t    if let Err(e) = main_result {\n404\t        error!(\"main server error: {}\", e);\n405\t    }\n406\t    if let Err(e) = metrics_result {\n407\t        error!(\"metrics server error: {}\", e);\n408\t    }\n409\t\n410\t    Ok(())\n411\t}\n412\t\n413\t/// Background health checker - promotes nodes to Active when reachable.\n414\t///\n415\t/// On each tick it also updates the Prometheus metrics for node health,\n416\t/// shard coverage, shard distribution, and degraded shard count.\n417\tasync fn run_health_checker(state: admin_endpoints::AppState) {\n418\t    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n419\t        state.config.health.interval_ms,\n420\t    ));\n421\t\n422\t    loop {\n423\t        interval.tick().await;\n424\t\n425\t        let mut topo = state.topology.write().await;\n426\t        let mut all_healthy = true;\n427\t\n428\t        // Collect node IDs to iterate\n429\t        let node_ids: Vec<_> = topo.nodes().map(|n| n.id.clone()).collect();\n430\t\n431\t        for node_id in &node_ids {\n432\t            // Get current node status\n433\t            let current_status = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.status);\n434\t\n435\t            // Skip nodes that are already Active/Healthy\n436\t            if let Some(NodeStatus::Active) | Some(NodeStatus::Healthy) = current_status {\n437\t                continue;\n438\t            }\n439\t\n440\t            // Get node address\n441\t            let node_address = match topo.node(node_id) {\n442\t                Some(n) => n.address.clone(),\n443\t                None => {\n444\t                    all_healthy = false;\n445\t                    continue;\n446\t                }\n447\t            };\n448\t\n449\t            // Try to reach the node\n450\t            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n451\t                .timeout(Duration::from_millis(state.config.health.timeout_ms))\n452\t                .build()\n453\t            {\n454\t                Ok(c) => c,\n455\t                Err(_) => {\n456\t                    all_healthy = false;\n457\t                    continue;\n458\t                }\n459\t            };\n460\t\n461\t            let url = format!(\"{}/health\", node_address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n462\t            let result = client.get(&url).send().await;\n463\t\n464\t            if result.is_ok() && result.unwrap().status().is_success() {\n465\t                // Node is reachable - promote to Active\n466\t                if let Some(node) = topo.node_mut(node_id) {\n467\t                    let _ = node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active);\n468\t                    info!(node_id = %node_id, \"node promoted to Active\");\n469\t                }\n470\t            } else {\n471\t                all_healthy = false;\n472\t            }\n473\t        }\n474\t\n475\t        // Update node health gauges (§10 node metrics)\n476\t        for node_id in &node_ids {\n477\t            let healthy = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.is_healthy()).unwrap_or(false);\n478\t            state.metrics.set_node_healthy(node_id.as_str(), healthy);\n479\t        }\n480\t\n481\t        // Compute and update shard metrics (§10 shard metrics)\n482\t        update_shard_metrics(&topo, &state.metrics);\n483\t\n484\t        // Update task registry size gauge\n485\t        let task_count = state.task_registry.count();\n486\t        state.metrics.set_task_registry_size(task_count as f64);\n487\t\n488\t        // Mark ready once all configured nodes are reachable\n489\t        if all_healthy && !state.config.nodes.is_empty() {\n490\t            state.mark_ready().await;\n491\t        }\n492\t\n493\t        // Update §14.9 resource-pressure metrics\n494\t        update_resource_pressure_metrics(&state.metrics);\n495\t    }\n496\t}\n497\t\n498\t/// Compute shard coverage, degraded count, and per-node shard distribution\n499\t/// from the current topology and update the corresponding Prometheus gauges.\n500\tfn update_shard_metrics(topo: &Topology, metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n501\t    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n502\t    let mut healthy_shards = 0u64;\n503\t    let mut degraded_shards = 0u64;\n504\t\n505\t    // Per-node shard count\n506\t    let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<miroir_core::topology::NodeId, u64> =\n507\t        std::collections::HashMap::new();\n508\t\n509\t    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n510\t        let mut has_healthy_replica = false;\n511\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n512\t            let assigned = miroir_core::router::assign_shard_in_group(\n513\t                shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf(),\n514\t            );\n515\t            for node_id in &assigned {\n516\t                let healthy = node_map\n517\t                    .get(node_id)\n518\t                    .map(|n| n.is_healthy())\n519\t                    .unwrap_or(false);\n520\t                if healthy {\n521\t                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n522\t                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n523\t                }\n524\t            }\n525\t        }\n526\t        if has_healthy_replica {\n527\t            healthy_shards += 1;\n528\t        } else {\n529\t            degraded_shards += 1;\n530\t        }\n531\t    }\n532\t\n533\t    let coverage = if topo.shards > 0 {\n534\t        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n535\t    } else {\n536\t        1.0\n537\t    };\n538\t    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n539\t    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n540\t\n541\t    for (node_id, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n542\t        metrics.set_shard_distribution(node_id.as_str(), *count as f64);\n543\t    }\n544\t}\n545\t\n546\t/// Read cgroup v2 memory pressure and update §14.9 resource-pressure gauges.\n547\t///\n548\t/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are the\n549\t/// standard cgroup v2 mount points. If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n550\t/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\n551\tfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n552\t    // ── Memory pressure ──\n553\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n554\t    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n555\t    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n556\t\n557\t    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n558\t        if max > 0 {\n559\t            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n560\t            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n561\t            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n562\t        }\n563\t    }\n564\t\n565\t    // ── CPU throttling ──\n566\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat contains throttle_usec\n567\t    if let Ok(contents) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat\") {\n568\t        for line in contents.lines() {\n569\t            if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"throttled_usec \") {\n570\t                if let Ok(usec) = rest.trim().parse::<f64>() {\n571\t                    // Report delta — the counter is cumulative, so we report\n572\t                    // the raw value and let Prometheus handle rate().\n573\t                    // For simplicity we set the counter to the absolute value\n574\t                    // (Prometheus counters are monotonic; since this is called\n575\t                    // periodically, we just inc by the new delta).\n576\t                    // Actually, the metric is a Counter, so we can only inc it.\n577\t                    // We'll read the previous throttled value from a thread-local.\n578\t                    // Simpler approach: just report the current throttle time\n579\t                    // as a one-shot increment if non-zero.\n580\t                    metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(usec / 1_000_000.0);\n581\t                }\n582\t            }\n583\t        }\n584\t    }\n585\t\n586\t    // ── Peer pod count and leader status ──\n587\t    // In the current single-pod or HA-proxy model, peer count = configured nodes\n588\t    // that are healthy. Leader is always true for the active pod (no election yet).\n589\t    // These will be refined when peer discovery (§14.3) lands.\n590\t    metrics.set_peer_pod_count(1);\n591\t    metrics.set_leader(true);\n592\t    metrics.set_owned_shards_count(0);\n593\t}\n594\t\n595\t/// Read a single integer value from a cgroup pseudo-file.\n596\tfn read_cgroup_metric(path: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n597\t    std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n598\t        .ok()\n599\t        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())\n600\t}\n601\t\n602\t/// Graceful shutdown signal handler.\n603\tasync fn shutdown_signal() {\n604\t    let ctrl_c = async {\n605\t        signal::ctrl_c()\n606\t            .await\n607\t            .expect(\"failed to install Ctrl+C handler\");\n608\t    };\n609\t\n610\t    #[cfg(unix)]\n611\t    let terminate = async {\n612\t        signal::unix::signal(signal::unix::SignalKind::terminate())\n613\t            .expect(\"failed to install signal handler\")\n614\t            .recv()\n615\t            .await;\n616\t    };\n617\t\n618\t    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n619\t    let terminate = std::future::pending::<()>;\n620\t\n621\t    tokio::select! {\n622\t        _ = ctrl_c => {},\n623\t        _ = terminate => {},\n624\t    }\n625\t\n626\t    info!(\"shutdown signal received, draining in-flight requests...\");\n627\t\n628\t    // Shutdown OpenTelemetry to flush any pending traces\n629\t    otel::shutdown_otel();\n630\t}\n631\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b7a424fa-907b-4676-bfa5-f01466faa86f","uuid":"0b5e51a9-2cf7-4a5b-8361-39c7f2096916","timestamp":"2026-05-04T00:21:36.873Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/main.rs","content":"use state.query_capture.clone(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[tokio::main]\nasync fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    // Load configuration (file → env → CLI overlay)\n    let config = MiroirConfig::load()\n        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Failed to load config: {}\", e))?;\n\n    // Initialize structured JSON logging (plan §10 format)\n    // Fields on every line: timestamp, level, target, message, pod_id\n    // Per-request fields (request_id) are added by telemetry middleware span.\n    let filter = EnvFilter::try_from_default_env()\n        .unwrap_or_else(|_| EnvFilter::new(\"info\"));\n\n    let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n\n    // Build subscriber - conditionally add OTel layer\n    // Note: We rebuild the layers in each branch because the types differ\n    // OTel layer must be applied to the bare registry first\n    if let Some(otel_layer) = otel::init_otel_layer(&config) {\n        let json_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n            .json()\n            .flatten_event(true)\n            .with_target(true)\n            .with_current_span(true)\n            .with_span_list(false);\n        // Apply OTel layer to registry first, then add filter and json layer\n        registry()\n            .with(otel_layer)\n            .with(filter)\n            .with(json_layer)\n            .init();\n    } else {\n        let json_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n            .json()\n            .flatten_event(true)\n            .with_target(true)\n            .with_current_span(true)\n            .with_span_list(false);\n        registry()\n            .with(filter)\n            .with(json_layer)\n            .init();\n    }\n\n    // Set pod_id as a global default field so it appears on every log line.\n    // This is done via a separate info span that is entered once and never\n    // left — its fields propagate to all child spans and events.\n    let _pod_span = tracing::info_span!(\"runtime\", pod_id = %pod_id).entered();\n\n    info!(\n        shards = config.shards,\n        replication_factor = config.replication_factor,\n        replica_groups = config.replica_groups,\n        \"miroir-proxy starting\"\n    );\n\n    // Validate critical secrets at startup (plan §9: \"orchestrator refuses to\n    // start the search UI without it\").\n    if config.search_ui.enabled {\n        let jwt_env = &config.search_ui.auth.jwt_secret_env;\n        match std::env::var(jwt_env) {\n            Ok(v) if !v.is_empty() => {}\n            _ => {\n                anyhow::bail!(\n                    \"search_ui is enabled but {} is not set — refusing to start. \\\n                     Either set the env var or disable search_ui (search_ui.enabled: false)\",\n                    jwt_env\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Build unified state\n    let state = UnifiedState::new(config.clone());\n\n    // Start health checker background task\n    let health_checker_state = state.admin.clone();\n    tokio::spawn(async move {\n        run_health_checker(health_checker_state).await;\n    });\n\n    // Start scoped key rotation background task (requires Redis)\n    if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        let rotation_state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n            config: state.admin.config.clone(),\n            redis: redis.clone(),\n            pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n        };\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            scoped_key_rotation::run_scoped_key_rotator(rotation_state).await;\n        });\n\n        // Start admin session revocation Pub/Sub subscriber (plan §9).\n        // When any pod revokes a session (logout), the session ID is published\n        // to `miroir:admin_session:revoked`. Every pod subscribes and adds the\n        // ID to its in-memory DashMap, ensuring revoked cookies are rejected\n        // across all pods within milliseconds.\n        let revoked_sessions = state.auth.revoked_sessions.clone();\n        let revoked_total = state.auth.admin_session_revoked_total.clone();\n        let redis_url = config.task_store.url.clone();\n        let key_prefix = redis.key_prefix().to_string();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            info!(\"starting admin session revocation subscriber\");\n            if let Err(e) = miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore::subscribe_session_revocations(\n                &redis_url,\n                &key_prefix,\n                move |session_id: String| {\n                    revoked_sessions.insert(session_id, ());\n                    revoked_total.inc();\n                },\n            )\n            .await\n            {\n                error!(error = %e, \"admin session revocation subscriber exited with error\");\n            }\n        });\n    }\n\n    // Build the main app router with UnifiedState\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\"/health\", get(health::get_health))\n        .route(\"/version\", get(version::get_version::<UnifiedState>))\n        .route(\"/stats\", get(indexes::global_stats_handler))\n        .route(\"/multi-search\", post(multi_search::multi_search::<UnifiedState>)) // §13.11\n        .nest(\"/_miroir\", admin::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/indexes\", indexes::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/keys\", keys::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/search\", search::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/settings\", settings::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/tasks\", tasks::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        // IMPORTANT: Layer order matters! Last layer() call = outermost = runs first.\n        // The middleware stack (from outermost to innermost):\n        // 1. csrf_middleware - runs first\n        // 2. auth_middleware\n        // 3. Extension layers\n        // 4. request_id_middleware - sets X-Request-Id header\n        // 5. telemetry_middleware - reads X-Request-Id, creates tracing span with request_id field\n        //    The span's request_id field propagates to all child log events via with_current_span(true)\n        //\n        // To achieve this order, we add layers in REVERSE (last call = outermost):\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            TelemetryState {\n                metrics: state.metrics.clone(),\n                pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n            },\n            middleware::telemetry_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n            middleware::request_id_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::extract::DefaultBodyLimit::max(\n            config.server.max_body_bytes as usize,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::Extension(state.admin.config.clone()))\n        .layer(axum::Extension(std::sync::Arc::new(state.admin.clone())))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state.auth.clone(),\n            auth::auth_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            auth::CsrfState {\n                auth: state.auth.clone(),\n                redis_store: state.redis_store.clone(),\n            },\n            auth::csrf_middleware,\n        ))\n        .with_state(state.clone());\n\n    let main_addr: SocketAddr = format!(\"{}:{}\", config.server.bind, config.server.port)\n        .parse()\n        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Invalid bind address: {}\", e))?;\n    let metrics_addr: SocketAddr = SocketAddr::from(([0, 0, 0, 0], 9090));\n\n    info!(\n        main_addr = %main_addr,\n        metrics_addr = %metrics_addr,\n        \"listening\"\n    );\n\n    // Create listeners\n    let main_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(main_addr).await?;\n    let metrics_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(metrics_addr).await?;\n\n    // Spawn main server with graceful shutdown\n    let main_server = axum::serve(main_listener, app)\n        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n\n    // Spawn metrics server with graceful shutdown\n    let metrics_app = metrics_router().with_state(state.metrics.clone());\n    let metrics_server = axum::serve(metrics_listener, metrics_app)\n        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n\n    // Run both servers concurrently\n    let (main_result, metrics_result) = tokio::join!(\n        main_server,\n        metrics_server\n    );\n\n    // Check for errors\n    if let Err(e) = main_result {\n        error!(\"main server error: {}\", e);\n    }\n    if let Err(e) = metrics_result {\n        error!(\"metrics server error: {}\", e);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Background health checker - promotes nodes to Active when reachable.\n///\n/// On each tick it also updates the Prometheus metrics for node health,\n/// shard coverage, shard distribution, and degraded shard count.\nasync fn run_health_checker(state: admin_endpoints::AppState) {\n    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n        state.config.health.interval_ms,\n    ));\n\n    loop {\n        interval.tick().await;\n\n        let mut topo = state.topology.write().await;\n        let mut all_healthy = true;\n\n        // Collect node IDs to iterate\n        let node_ids: Vec<_> = topo.nodes().map(|n| n.id.clone()).collect();\n\n        for node_id in &node_ids {\n            // Get current node status\n            let current_status = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.status);\n\n            // Skip nodes that are already Active/Healthy\n            if let Some(NodeStatus::Active) | Some(NodeStatus::Healthy) = current_status {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Get node address\n            let node_address = match topo.node(node_id) {\n                Some(n) => n.address.clone(),\n                None => {\n                    all_healthy = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            // Try to reach the node\n            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n                .timeout(Duration::from_millis(state.config.health.timeout_ms))\n                .build()\n            {\n                Ok(c) => c,\n                Err(_) => {\n                    all_healthy = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            let url = format!(\"{}/health\", node_address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n            let result = client.get(&url).send().await;\n\n            if result.is_ok() && result.unwrap().status().is_success() {\n                // Node is reachable - promote to Active\n                if let Some(node) = topo.node_mut(node_id) {\n                    let _ = node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active);\n                    info!(node_id = %node_id, \"node promoted to Active\");\n                }\n            } else {\n                all_healthy = false;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Update node health gauges (§10 node metrics)\n        for node_id in &node_ids {\n            let healthy = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.is_healthy()).unwrap_or(false);\n            state.metrics.set_node_healthy(node_id.as_str(), healthy);\n        }\n\n        // Compute and update shard metrics (§10 shard metrics)\n        update_shard_metrics(&topo, &state.metrics);\n\n        // Update task registry size gauge\n        let task_count = state.task_registry.count();\n        state.metrics.set_task_registry_size(task_count as f64);\n\n        // Mark ready once all configured nodes are reachable\n        if all_healthy && !state.config.nodes.is_empty() {\n            state.mark_ready().await;\n        }\n\n        // Update §14.9 resource-pressure metrics\n        update_resource_pressure_metrics(&state.metrics);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compute shard coverage, degraded count, and per-node shard distribution\n/// from the current topology and update the corresponding Prometheus gauges.\nfn update_shard_metrics(topo: &Topology, metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n    let mut healthy_shards = 0u64;\n    let mut degraded_shards = 0u64;\n\n    // Per-node shard count\n    let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<miroir_core::topology::NodeId, u64> =\n        std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n        let mut has_healthy_replica = false;\n        for group in topo.groups() {\n            let assigned = miroir_core::router::assign_shard_in_group(\n                shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf(),\n            );\n            for node_id in &assigned {\n                let healthy = node_map\n                    .get(node_id)\n                    .map(|n| n.is_healthy())\n                    .unwrap_or(false);\n                if healthy {\n                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        if has_healthy_replica {\n            healthy_shards += 1;\n        } else {\n            degraded_shards += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    let coverage = if topo.shards > 0 {\n        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n    } else {\n        1.0\n    };\n    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n\n    for (node_id, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n        metrics.set_shard_distribution(node_id.as_str(), *count as f64);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Read cgroup v2 memory pressure and update §14.9 resource-pressure gauges.\n///\n/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are the\n/// standard cgroup v2 mount points. If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\nfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n    // ── Memory pressure ──\n    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n\n    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n        if max > 0 {\n            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── CPU throttling ──\n    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat contains throttle_usec\n    if let Ok(contents) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat\") {\n        for line in contents.lines() {\n            if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"throttled_usec \") {\n                if let Ok(usec) = rest.trim().parse::<f64>() {\n                    // Report delta — the counter is cumulative, so we report\n                    // the raw value and let Prometheus handle rate().\n                    // For simplicity we set the counter to the absolute value\n                    // (Prometheus counters are monotonic; since this is called\n                    // periodically, we just inc by the new delta).\n                    // Actually, the metric is a Counter, so we can only inc it.\n                    // We'll read the previous throttled value from a thread-local.\n                    // Simpler approach: just report the current throttle time\n                    // as a one-shot increment if non-zero.\n                    metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(usec / 1_000_000.0);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── Peer pod count and leader status ──\n    // In the current single-pod or HA-proxy model, peer count = configured nodes\n    // that are healthy. Leader is always true for the active pod (no election yet).\n    // These will be refined when peer discovery (§14.3) lands.\n    metrics.set_peer_pod_count(1);\n    metrics.set_leader(true);\n    metrics.set_owned_shards_count(0);\n}\n\n/// Read a single integer value from a cgroup pseudo-file.\nfn read_cgroup_metric(path: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n    std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n        .ok()\n        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())\n}\n\n/// Graceful shutdown signal handler.\nasync fn shutdown_signal() {\n    let ctrl_c = async {\n        signal::ctrl_c()\n            .await\n            .expect(\"failed to install Ctrl+C handler\");\n    };\n\n    #[cfg(unix)]\n    let terminate = async {\n        signal::unix::signal(signal::unix::SignalKind::terminate())\n            .expect(\"failed to install signal handler\")\n            .recv()\n            .await;\n    };\n\n    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n    let terminate = std::future::pending::<()>;\n\n    tokio::select! {\n        _ = ctrl_c => {},\n        _ = terminate => {},\n    }\n\n    info!(\"shutdown signal received, draining in-flight requests...\");\n\n    // Shutdown OpenTelemetry to flush any pending traces\n    otel::shutdown_otel();\n}\n","numLines":631,"startLine":1,"totalLines":631}}}

Uuid::now_v7();\n        let bytes = uuid.as_bytes();\n        // Hash the full UUID to ensure uniqueness even within the same millisecond\n        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n        hasher.write(bytes);\n        let hash = hasher.finish();\n        // Take first 8 hex chars of 64-bit hash (32 bits is sufficient entropy)\n        Self(format!(\"{:08x}\", hash as u32))\n    }\n\n    /// Get the inner request ID string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a RequestId from a string.\n    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n        if s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()) {\n            Some(Self(s))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\npub async fn request_id_middleware(\n    mut req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    // Check for existing request ID in headers\n    let request_id = req\n        .headers()\n        .get(\"x-request-id\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| RequestId::parse(s.to_string()))\n        .unwrap_or_else(RequestId::new);\n\n    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n    req.extensions_mut().insert(request_id.clone());\n\n    // Set X-Request-Id header on request (for telemetry_middleware to read)\n    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n        req.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n    }\n\n    // Process the request\n    let mut response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    // Add X-Request-Id header to response (override if exists)\n    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n        response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n    }\n\n    response\n}\n\n\n/// Telemetry state combining metrics and pod_id for middleware.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct TelemetryState {\n    pub metrics: Metrics,\n    pub pod_id: String,\n}\n","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1920}}}

&PreflightRequest,\n383\t    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n384\t        let start = Instant::now();\n385\t        let base = address.trim_end_matches('/');\n386\t\n387\t        tracing::debug!(\n388\t            target: \"miroir.node\",\n389\t            node_id = %node,\n390\t            address = %address,\n391\t            index = %request.index_uid,\n392\t            operation = \"preflight\",\n393\t            term_count = request.terms.len(),\n394\t            \"node call started\"\n395\t        );\n396\t\n397\t        // 1. Get DF for each term via search with limit=0\n427\t        let mut term_stats = HashMap::new();\n428\t        let search_url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", base, request.index_uid);\n429\t        for term in &request.terms {\n430\t            let search_body = serde_json::json!({\"q\": term, \"limit\": 0});\n431\t\n432\t            let search_resp = self\n433\t                .client\n434\t                .post(&search_url)\n435\t                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n436\t                .json(&search_body)\n437\t                .send()\n438\t                .await\n439\t                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"DF search failed: {}\", e)))?;\n440\t\n441\t            if search_resp.status().is_success() {\n442\t                let body: Value = search_resp\n443\t                    .json()\n444\t                    .await\n445\t                    .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse DF response: {}\", e)))?;\n446\t                let df = body\n447\t                    .get(\"estimatedTotalHits\")\n448\t                    .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n449\t                    .unwrap_or(0);\n450\t                term_stats.insert(term.clone(), TermStats { df });\n451\t            }\n452\t        }\n453\t\n454\t        // 3. Estimate avg doc length (Meilisearch doesn't expose this directly;\n455\t        //    use a default. The BM25 score is mainly sensitive to IDF, not avgdl.)\n456\t        let avg_doc_length = 500.0;\n457\t\n458\t        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n459\t        tracing::debug!(\n460\t            target: \"miroir.node\",\n461\t            node_id = %node,\n462\t            address = %address,\n463\t            operation = \"preflight\",\n464\t            duration_ms,\n465\t            total_docs,\n466\t            \"node call completed\"\n467\t        );\n468\t\n469\t        Ok(PreflightResponse {\n470\t            total_docs,\n471\t            avg_doc_length,\n472\t            term_stats,\n473\t        })\n474\t    }\n475\t\n476\t    fn get_task_status(\n477\t        &self,\n478\t        node: &NodeId,\n479\t        address: &str,\n480\t span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"node_call\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            operation = \"search\",\n            index = %request.index_uid,\n        );\n        let _guard = span.enter();\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let url = self.search_url(address, &request.index_uid);\n\n        let mut body = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            operation = \"delete_by_filter\",\n            duration_ms,\n            status = status.as_u16(),\n            \"node call completed\"\n        );\n\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            // Try to parse as Meilisearch error\n            if let Ok(meili_err) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&body_text) {\n                return Ok(DeleteResponse {\n                    success: false,\n                    task_uid: None,\n                    message: meili_err.get(\"message\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                    code: meili_err.get(\"code\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                    error_type: meili_err.get(\"type\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                });\n            }\n            return Err(NodeError::HttpError {\n                status: status.as_u16(),\n                body: body_text,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Parse successful response\n        let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n            NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse JSON response: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid: json.get(\"taskUid\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()),\n            message: None,\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let base = address.trim_end_matches('/');\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            index = %request.index_uid,\n            operation = \"preflight\",\n            term_count = request.terms.len(),\n            \"node call started\"\n        );\n\n        // 1. Get total docs from Meilisearch stats endpoint\n        let stats_url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/stats\", base, request.index_uid);\n        let stats_resp = self\n            .client\n            .get(&stats_url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Stats request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        if !stats_resp.status().is_success() {\n            // Index not found or node unreachable — return empty stats\n            return Ok(PreflightResponse {\n                total_docs: 0,\n                avg_doc_length: 0.0,\n                term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n            });\n        }\n\n        let stats_body: Value = stats_resp\n            .json()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse stats: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let total_docs = stats_body\n            .get(\"numberOfDocuments\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n            .unwrap_or(0);\n\n        // 2. Get DF for each term via search with limit=0\n        let mut term_stats = HashMap::new();\n        let search_url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", base, request.index_uid);\n        for term in &request.terms {\n            let search_body = serde_json::json!({\"q\": term, \"limit\": 0});\n\n            let search_resp = self\n                .client\n                .post(&search_url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n                .json(&search_body)\n                .send()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"DF search failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            if search_resp.status().is_success() {\n                let body: Value = search_resp\n                    .json()\n                    .await\n                    .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse DF response: {}\", e)))?;\n                let df = body\n                    .get(\"estimatedTotalHits\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n                    .unwrap_or(0);\n                term_stats.insert(term.clone(), TermStats { df });\n            }\n        }\n\n        // 3. Estimate avg doc length (Meilisearch doesn't expose this directly;\n        //    use a default. The BM25 score is mainly sensitive to IDF, not avgdl.)\n        let avg_doc_length = 500.0;\n\n        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            operation = \"preflight\",\n            duration_ms,\n            total_docs,\n            \"node call completed\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs,\n            avg_doc_length,\n            term_stats,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn get_task_status(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n        let task_uid = request.task_uid;\n        let url = Self::task_url_static(address, task_uid);\n        let master_key = self.master_key.clone();\n        let client = self.client.clone();\n\n        async move {\n            let response = client\n                .get(&url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", master_key))\n                .send()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            let status = response.status();\n            let body_text = response\n                .text()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to read response: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            if !status.is_success() {\n                return Err(NodeError::HttpError {\n                    status: status.as_u16(),\n                    body: body_text,\n                });\n            }\n\n            // Parse successful response\n            let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n                NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse JSON response: {}\", e))\n            })?;\n\n            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n                task_uid,\n                status: json.get(\"status\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .unwrap_or(\"enqueued\")\n                    .to_string(),\n                error: json.get(\"error\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.get(\"message\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                error_type: json.get(\"error\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.get(\"type\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .map(|s| s.to_string()),\n            })\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_url_construction() {\n        let client = HttpClient::new(\"test-key\".into(), 5000);\n        assert_eq!(\n            client.search_url(\"http://localhost:7700\", \"my_index\"),\n            \"http://localhost:7700/indexes/my_index/search\"\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            client.search_url(\"http://localhost:7700/\", \"my_index\"),\n            \"http://localhost:7700/indexes/my_index/search\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_preflight_url_construction() {\n        let client = HttpClient::new(\"test-key\".into(), 5000);\n        assert_eq!(\n            client.preflight_url(\"http://localhost:7700\", \"my_index\"),\n            \"http://localhost:7700/indexes/my_index/_preflight\"\n        );\n This handler implements the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n124\t/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n125\t///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n126\t/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n127\t///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n128\t///\n129\t/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n130\t/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; strips `_rankingScore` unless client\n131\t/// explicitly requested it.\n132\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\n133\tasync fn search_handler(\n134\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n135\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n136\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n137\t    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n138\t) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n139\t    let start = Instant::now();\n140\t    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n141\t\n142\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n143\t    let source_ip = headers\n144\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n145\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n146\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n147\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n148\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n149\t        .trim()\n150\t        .to_string();\n151\t\n152\t    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n153\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n154\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n155\t        Err(e) => {\n156\t            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n157\t            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n158\t        }\n159\t    };\n160\t\n161\t    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n162\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n163\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n164\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n165\t                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n166\t                    if !allowed {\n167\t                        warn!(\n168\t                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n169\t                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n170\t                        );\n171\t                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n172\t                    }\n173\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n174\t                }\n175\t                Err(e) => {\n176\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n177\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n178\t                }\n179\t            }\n180\t        }\n181\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n182\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n183\t            &source_ip,\n184\t            limit,\n185\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n186\t        );\n187\t        if !allowed {\n188\t            warn!(\n189\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n190\t                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n191\t            );\n192\t            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n193\t        }\n194\t    }\n195\t\n196\t    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n197\t    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n198\t    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n199\t    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n200\t        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&index) {\n201\t            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n202\t            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n203\t            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n204\t                &state.pod_id,\n205\t                &index,\n206\t                sk.generation,\n207\t            );\n208\t\n209\t            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n210\t            sk.primary_key\n211\t        } else {\n212\t            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n213\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n214\t        }\n215\t    } else {\n216\t        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n217\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n218\t    };\n219\t\n220\t    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n221\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n222\t    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n223\t        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n224\t        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n225\t        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n226\t        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n227\t    };\n228\t\n229\t    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n230\t    let plan = {\n231\t        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n232\t            \"scatter_plan\",\n233\t            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n234\t            shards = state.config.shards,\n235\t            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n236\t        ).entered();\n237\t        plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n238\t    };\n239\t    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n240\t\n241\t    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n242\t    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n243\t\n244\t    // Build search request\n245\t    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n246\t        index_uid: index.clone(),\n247\t        query: body.q,\n248\t        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n249\t        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n250\t        filter: body.filter,\n251\t        facets: (or node_master_key as fallback)\n258\t    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n259\t        search_key,\n260\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n261\t    ));\n262\t    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n263\t\n264\t    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n265\t    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n266\t\n267\t    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n268\t    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n269\t        plan,\n270\t        &client,\n271\t        search_req,\n272\t        &topo,\n273\t        policy,\n274\t        &strategy,\n275\t    )\n276\t    .await\n277\t    .map_err(|e| {\n278\t        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n279\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n280\t    })?;\n281\t\n282\t    // Drop topology lock before building response\n283\t    drop(topo);\n284\t\n285\t    // Strip internal fields from hits\n286\t    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n287\t        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n288\t    }\n289\t\n290\t    // Build response body\n291\t    let mut body = serde_json::json!({\n292\t        \"hits\": result.hits,\n293\t        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n294\t        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n295\t    });\n296\t\n297\t    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n298\t    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n299\t        body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n300\t    }\n301\t\n302\t    // Build response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header\n303\t    let mut response = Response::builder()\n304\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n305\t        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n306\t\n307\t    if result.degraded {\n308\t        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n309\t    }\n310\t\n311\t    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n312\t        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n313\t        sorted_shards.sort();\n314\t        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n315\t            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n316\t            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n317\t            .join(\",\");\n318\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n319\t    } else if result.degraded {\n320\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n321\t    }\n322\t\n323\t    let response = response\n324\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n325\t        .unwrap();\n326\t\n327\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n328\t    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n329\t    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n330\t    tracing::info!(\n331\t        target: \"miroir.search\",\n332\t        index = %index,\n333\t        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n334\t        node_count = node_count,\n335\t        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n336\t        degraded = result.degraded,\n337\t        \"search completed\"\n338\t    );\n339\t\n340\t    Ok(response)\n341\t}\n342\t\n343\t/// Strip `_miroir_shard` from all hits (always).\n344\t/// Strip `_rankingScore` unless the client explicitly requested it.\n345\tpub fn strip_internal_fields(hit: &mut Value, client_requested_score: bool) {\n346\t    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n347\t        obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n348\t        if !client_requested_score {\n349\t            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n350\t        }\n351\t    }\n352\t}\n353\t\n354\t#[cfg(test)]\n355\tmod tests {\n356\t    use super::*;\n357\t\n358\t    #[test]\n359\t    fn test_search_request_debug_redaction() {\n360\t        let body = SearchRequestBody
